type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Please Total PM10 is (0.7783)  ton this year. It is based  on our cooling operation in summer  and  heating load in summer. Our cooling  load is estimated 20 % from December to April,  April to Sept. is 55% and Sept to November 25% Cooling tower’s (PM10) is 0.011 ton. For boilers we   use natural gas and TPM all is 0.76763 ton.
 Our load   changes  little due to cooling load in summer and heating load in winter. So our TPM is almost constant year around.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Emission factors used in previous years included only published emission factors for large wall-fired boilers.  Quantification methodologies to estimate 2023 emissions include source testing results for Boiler Nos. 1 and 2, and published emission factors for diesel fuel combustion, natural gas combustion obtained from U.S. EPA AP-42.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Emission factors used in previous years included only published emission factors for large wall-fired boilers.  Quantification methodologies to estimate 2023 emissions include source testing results for Boiler No. 2, and published emission factors for diesel fuel combustion, natural gas combustion obtained from U.S. EPA AP-42.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: SO2 emissions were not reported for the 2022 NPRI submission.  SO2 emissions reported for 2023 were estimated using U.S. EPA AP-42 emission factors for diesel fuel combustion.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Emission factors used in previous years included only published emission factors.  Quantification methodologies to estimate 2023 emissions include source testing results for Boiler Nos. 1 and 2, and published emission factors for diesel fuel combustion, natural gas combustion and cooling tower releases obtained from U.S. EPA AP-42.
